title: Kernel subagent5 queue lane. Repository: /Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto. Narrow task: inspect post_queue.json semantics and give the safest way to add a new draft scheduled_for='2026-04-01 22:00' without violating x-auto rules, given that an approved item already occupies that slot. Do not write files....
summary_text: Current queue state confirms the slot is occupied by an `approved` item: - `2026-04-01 22:00` → `id=3316b8c0f0ac8103b66fc3368869cb7d`, `status=approved` in [post_queue.json:309](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/post_queue.json#L309). Safest semantics-based approach: 1. Add the new item as `draft` (not `approved`) in Notion-first flow. 2. Treat `2026-04-01 22:00` as temporary only; do not approve while the existing approved item keeps that slot. 3. Before approval, move the draft to a unique slot from `POST_SCHEDULE` (best: `2026-04-02 22:00` to keep the same hour). 4. Then approve. Why this is safest: - Duplicate schedule is enforced for `approved` posts only in audit/alerts: [queue_audit.py:108](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/queue_audit.py#L108), [queue_audit.py:128](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/queue_audit.py#L128), [scheduler.py:192](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L192). - Scheduler only posts `approved`; `draft` won’t post: [scheduler.py:224](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L224). - Queue SoT is Notion-first, so local-only edits are not the safe source of truth: [post_generator.py:256](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/post_generator.py#L256), [notion_sync.py:552](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L552). - `queue_audit --fix` is not ideal for this case because it resolves approved collisions by `+1 minute` shifts, not canonical slot times: [queue_audit.py:272](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/queue_audit.py#L272). So: same-slot draft is acceptable short-term, but safest conflict handling is “reschedule before approval.”
